name: ClickHouse MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official ClickHouse MCP server enabling AI agents to query ClickHouse databases — running SQL analytics, exploring table schemas, executing OLAP queries, and accessing ClickHouse's high-performance columnar storage for real-time analytics at scale.
use_cases: Running OLAP analytics queries from data analysis agents
Exploring table schemas and data structures for data discovery agents
Executing real-time aggregations over billions of rows
Querying time-series data for observability and monitoring agents
Building analytics dashboards by querying ClickHouse from agent pipelines
Analyzing product metrics and user behavior from event streams
not_for: Transactional workloads requiring row-level updates (use PostgreSQL, MySQL)
Teams using Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift
best_when: An agent needs to run high-performance SQL analytics on large datasets — especially for time-series, events, logs, or product metrics at scale.
avoid_when: You need transactional writes or row-level updates — ClickHouse is optimized for batch inserts and analytics reads.

auth.notes: Username/password with database-level and table-level permissions. Role-based access control. TLS certificates for secure connections. ClickHouse Cloud uses API key + hostname.

pricing.paid_tiers: ClickHouse Cloud: from $0.018/compute unit; storage separate; self-hosted free
pricing.notes: ClickHouse is open source — self-hosted is free. ClickHouse Cloud has consumption pricing. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: Self-imposed via max_concurrent_queries and query complexity settings

known_agent_gotchas: ClickHouse SQL has differences from standard SQL — use ClickHouse-specific functions
LIMIT is required for large tables — unbounded queries can return huge result sets
MergeTree family tables are eventually consistent — recent inserts may not be visible immediately
Mutations (UPDATE/DELETE) are async in ClickHouse — different from OLTP semantics
Distributed tables vs local tables — agents must know cluster topology
Time zone handling is critical for time-series queries
agent_readiness.security_notes: TLS enforced for ClickHouse Cloud. RBAC with database/table-level permissions. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR. Self-hosted security is operator responsibility.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (Cloud). RBAC with fine-grained permissions. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR. Self-hosted TLS is operator responsibility.
